decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing lands for control purposes, particularly through the interaction between Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage to create a powerful 20/20 token. The deck also aims to slow down the opponent's game plan with cards like Sphere of Resistance and Wasteland, while also maintaining a resource advantage through Life from the Loam and Exploration. Boseiju, Who Endures and Mox Diamond provide additional utility and mana acceleration, respectively. The inclusion of The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale and Ghost Quarter allows for disruption of the opponent's board and resources.

The key concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to quickly establish a powerful board presence using efficient Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Reality Smasher, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thorn of Amethyst. The deck aims to apply pressure early in the game, taking advantage of powerful mana acceleration provided by cards like Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal. By leveraging the Eldrazi tribe's synergies and aggressive creatures, the deck seeks to overwhelm opponents and secure a quick victory in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The main theme of this Bant Control deck is to establish control through a variety of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw effects. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually building up its own resources. The inclusion of cards like Leyline Binding and Quantum Riddler allows the player to lock down the opponent's threats, while cards like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide card advantage and selection. The deck also features a mix of powerful planeswalkers and efficient removal spells to deal with a variety of threats.

The focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly establish board presence with efficient creatures like Guide of Souls and Voice of Victory and disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Static Prison. The deck also includes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to provide additional threats and Goblin Bombardment for direct damage. The mana base is streamlined with fetch lands like Arid Mesa and Wasteland for land disruption. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure and control the game to secure a quick victory.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize fast mana acceleration cards like Dark Ritual and Lion's Eye Diamond to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to win the game. The deck aims to cast spells from the graveyard with Gaea's Will and utilize cards like Burning Wish and Beseech the Mirror to search for key cards like Tendrils of Agony or Song of Creation. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Hexing Squelcher to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The Fantasticar is a central card in the combo, allowing for explosive plays and granting access to powerful effects.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with cards like Gaea's Cradle and Mana Dorks, then utilizing powerful creatures like Wight of the Reliquary and Craterhoof Behemoth to overwhelm the opponent. It also includes disruptive creatures like Collector Ouphe and Endurance to control the opponent's board state. The deck is built around the synergy between mana generation and powerful creatures to create a strong and flexible control strategy that can quickly close out games.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by using a combination of hand disruption spells like Duress and Thoughtseize, along with counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation. In addition to disruption, the deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out. This allows the deck to maintain tempo and pressure the opponent with evasive threats like Murktide Regent. The mana base is designed to support the deck's two-color requirements while also including utility lands like Wasteland and Mystic Sanctuary.

The main concept of this deck is to maintain tempo and aggressive pressure on the opponent while controlling the board through counterspells and disruptive plays. Utilizing cheap and efficient spells like Brainstorm, Daze, Delver of Secrets, and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to quickly establish a board presence and chip away at the opponent's life total. Cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent provide additional threats, while Wasteland and disruptive spells like Force of Will help to keep the opponent off-balance and disrupt their game plan.

The key concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly establish a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Wasteland. The deck aims to apply pressure on the opponent using efficient creatures like Amped Raptor and Guide of Souls, while also having access to removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending. Additionally, the deck has some flexibility with cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment to close out the game or deal with problematic permanents.

The key concept of this deck is to establish board control and disrupt your opponent's game plan using disruptive creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Skyclave Apparition while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Stoneforge Mystic. Utilizing Aether Vial to cheat creatures into play and equipment like Umezawa's Jitte to boost your creatures' power is essential to winning the game. Additionally, the deck aims to utilize efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares to deal with threats and protect your creatures.

The main focus of this deck is to maintain tempo by combining aggressive creatures with disruption and removal spells. By utilizing cards like Fatal Push, Thoughtseize, and Wasteland, the deck aims to control the game while continually applying pressure through creatures like Orcish Bowmasters, Murktide Regent, and Nethergoyf. Using Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Brainstorm for card advantage and selection, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with efficient threats and disruptive spells, ultimately leading to victory through a combination of aggression and control.

The deck focuses on assembling the Doomsday combo by quickly digging through the deck with cards like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Thoughtseize. The key strategy is to use cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate mana and cast Doomsday, followed by using Thassa's Oracle to win the game by controlling the top of the library. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Daze to protect the combo. Additionally, Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and control, making the deck resilient against opposing strategies.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Eldrazi Confluence. By using fast mana sources like Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats like Thought-Knot Seer and Reality Smasher. With a mixture of disruption and efficient creatures, the Eldrazi Aggro deck aims to close out games quickly and efficiently in the Legacy format.

The main idea of this deck is to control the opponent's plays while ramping up mana quickly. By using cards like Expedition Map and Grim Monolith to search for lands such as Cloudpost and Tron lands, the deck can accelerate into big threats like Ugin, the One Ring, and Karn, the Great Creator. Force of Negation and Force of Will are used to protect key pieces, while Sink into Stupor and Kozilek's Command disrupt the opponent's strategy. The goal is to establish a dominant board presence and overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells and creatures.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through powerful spell interaction and disrupting opponents' strategies. It includes cards like Force of Will, Counterbalance, and Spell Pierce to counter important spells, while also utilizing cards like Swords to Plowshares and Terminus for removal. The deck aims to manipulate the top of the library with Brainstorm, Predict, and Thassa's Oracle to ensure consistency and advantage. Teferi, Time Raveler and Jace, Wielder of Mysteries provide additional card advantage and win conditions. The deck utilizes a combination of blue and white mana sources to support its control strategy.

The focus of this deck is to control the opponent's board and disrupt their game plan using counterspells like Force of Will and Daze, while also utilizing powerful removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The deck also aims to manipulate card advantage with cards like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, while also utilizing cards like Quantum Riddler to pressure the opponent. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind them out in a longer game, utilizing cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Karakas to disrupt their strategy.
